reasoning:
The threshold value on 2025-11-21 (the last business day before 2025-11-23) was 0.55. 

For the short-term horizon (2025-11-30), the FRED statistical model linear trend over the last month projects a continued slight downward drift, estimating a 33% probability of exceeding 0.55 on Nov 30. Despite a small upward momentum in the last 5 days (moving from 0.52 to 0.55), the short-term signal suggests a lack of strong upside conviction. Thus, P1 is estimated at 0.35, closely tracking the FRED statistical anchor.

For the medium-term horizon (2025-12-23), expert consensus (e.g., Reuters poll from Nov 13) anticipates standard yield curve steepening by year-end, predicting a spread around 60 basis points. As the time horizon expands to one month, variance increases, causing our probability to regress closer to 50%. Incorporating the slight expected positive drift from the consensus against the recent short-term negative trend gives an estimate of 0.44.

For the longer-term horizon (2026-02-21), standard deviation increases significantly (~15-20 bps over 3 months). Analysts predict the 2-year and 10-year yield dynamics (rate cuts vs. inflation pressures) will widen the spread to roughly 60-70 bps. This structural steepening expectation gives a mildly positive expected return relative to 0.55, pushing the probability slightly above 50% to 0.52. 

The probability estimates explicitly adhere to the instruction that uncertainty should increase over time, moving strictly closer to 0.50 for distant dates: |0.35 - 0.5| > |0.44 - 0.5| > |0.52 - 0.5|.
